verses.text: 'Praise waiteth for thee, O God, in Sion,' the God who hears prayer, to whom 'all flesh' shall come. He is the hearer of prayer. The God who answers His people will at last be sought by all the nations.

'Praise waiteth for thee, O God, in Sion,' the God who hears prayer, to whom 'all flesh' shall come. He is the hearer of prayer. The God who answers His people will at last be sought by all the nations.
Though iniquities prevail, 'thou shalt purge them away,' and blessed is the one God chooses to dwell near Him. Sin is met by pardon. The chosen are satisfied with the goodness of God's house.
Though iniquities prevail, 'thou shalt purge them away,' and blessed is the one God chooses to dwell near Him. Sin is met by pardon. The chosen are satisfied with the goodness of God's house.

By awesome deeds in righteousness God answers, the confidence of all the earth, who stills the roaring seas and the tumult of the peoples. His power steadies creation and nations alike. The God of the storm is the refuge of the trembling.

By awesome deeds in righteousness God answers, the confidence of all the earth, who stills the roaring seas and the tumult of the peoples. His power steadies creation and nations alike. The God of the storm is the refuge of the trembling.

By awesome deeds in righteousness God answers, the confidence of all the earth, who stills the roaring seas and the tumult of the peoples. His power steadies creation and nations alike. The God of the storm is the refuge of the trembling.

By awesome deeds in righteousness God answers, the confidence of all the earth, who stills the roaring seas and the tumult of the peoples. His power steadies creation and nations alike. The God of the storm is the refuge of the trembling.
He visits the earth and waters it, filling the river of God and enriching the furrows for grain. He tends the soil Himself. Every harvest is the work of God's watering hand.
He visits the earth and waters it, filling the river of God and enriching the furrows for grain. He tends the soil Himself. Every harvest is the work of God's watering hand.

'Thou crownest the year with thy goodness,' the pastures and valleys clothed with flocks and corn, shouting and singing. Creation rejoices in His bounty. The fruitful seasons are God's gifts, calling the earth to praise.
